title: training-job values
description: The complete deploy interface for an R&D training workload. Strict by design: additionalProperties is false everywhere, so an unknown key fails validation with a named field (R10). This schema is the documentation (R11). The R4 symmetry twin of golden-service — same values+schema contract, different (batch Job) shape.

team.description: Owning team; becomes the team.synorg.io/name label. GPU pods without it are denied by policy.

queue.description: Kueue LocalQueue in the team namespace (conventions.md: team-<name>); becomes the kueue.x-k8s.io/queue-name label. Admission flows LocalQueue -> training-borrow ClusterQueue.

gpu.description: GPUs per worker pod; becomes nvidia.com/gpu in limits. Training is always GPU work, so the minimum is 1 (unlike serving, which allows 0).

workers.description: Number of worker pods (Job parallelism/completions, Indexed). Each pod is one torchrun-elastic node; JOB_COMPLETION_INDEX is the node rank.

repository.description: Image repository, e.g. ghcr.io/nycterent/synorg/team/trainer. The entrypoint MUST resume from the latest checkpoint under CHECKPOINT_DIR.

tag.description: Immutable image tag; also published as app.kubernetes.io/version.

digest.description: Optional content digest for reproducible pinning. When set, the image deploys as repository:tag@digest so the exact bytes are enforced no matter what the tag later points at (the tag stays for the version label). Produce it in CI, e.g. `crane digest <repo>:<tag>`.

command.description: Container command override. Defaults to a torchrun-elastic scaffold; override to point at your training entrypoint while keeping elastic restart + checkpoint resume.

checkpoint.description: The KTD12 preemption contract: checkpoint often, resume from latest, so a preemption loses at most one interval of progress.

dir.description: Mount path the trainer writes checkpoints to (backed by the shared checkpoint store, infra/.../checkpoint-store). Published as CHECKPOINT_DIR; the entrypoint resumes from the latest checkpoint here.

intervalSeconds.description: Checkpoint cadence. Published as CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_SECONDS. Capped at 300 (5 min): the schema enforces the <=5 min lost-work budget (KTD12) — a slower cadence is rejected by name.

backoffLimit.description: Job backoffLimit. Preemption deletes a pod (a failure), so this must be generous enough to absorb the expected preemptions across a run; Kueue re-admits when the borrowing curve has headroom again.
